signals.evidence_excerpt: REORGANIZATION MEETING OF THE AVON PLANNING BOARD JANUARY 25, 2022 & REGULAR MEETING This meeting was held virtually through Zoom Attendance: Mayor Ed Bonanno, Commissioner Robert Mahon, Chair Richard Maloney, Vice Chair Teresa Egan, John Ryan, Vincent Ernst, Cal Talmage, Mike Davey, Marie Kenny, Sandra McLaughlin, Frank McGovern, Steve Mazouat, Attorney Mark Kitrick, Engineer Ray Savacool Absent: Joe Oldakowski Chair Maloney opens the meeting at 7PM, reads the opening statement in compliance with the Open Public Meetings Law and leads the Pledge to Allegiance to the Flag. Board attorney Mark Kitrick swears in Planning Board appointees for new terms. Steve Mazouat Alt. # 4 2 Years End 12/31/2024 RESOLUTION offered by, Maloney second by Bonanno , Whereas, the Open Public Meetings Law (CH 231, P.L. 1975) became effective on January 19, 1976, and Whereas, in accordance with said act, it is necessary for the Borough of Avon-By-The- Sea Planning Board, as a public body, to post and maintain a schedule of the regular meetings of the public body to be held during the succeeding year. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the Secretary of the Planning Board is hereby authorized to do the following: 1. Post the schedule of meetings on the bulletin board in Borough Hall, which sets the time, date and location of all regular and workshop meetings. The public as always will be allowed to participate in the regular meetings and a portion of the meetings will be set aside for public comment. 2. Mail a copy of the schedule to the official papers. 3. Each regular meeting of the Planning Board of the Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea shall convene at 7 PM. The location of all meetings are held at the Borough Gym 301 Main Street, however during the declared public health emergency (COVID 19) all meetings will be held by means of electronic communications equipment (ZOOM) until otherwise directed by the Governor of New Jersey. And adopted by roll call vote: AYE: Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO: 0 MOTION PASSED Resolution offered by Maloney second by Bonanno that the Asbury Park Press or The Coast Star will be the official newspaper for advertising for the Avon-By-The-Sea Planning Board for the year 2022. And adopted by roll call vote: AYE: Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO: 0 MOTION PASSED MOTION to adopt Resolution offered by Bonanno second by McGovern that Richard Maloney be the Chairperson of the Avon Planning Board beginning on January 1, 2022 and ending on the first meeting of 2023. And adopted by the following vote: AYE : Bonanno, Mahon, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO: 0 MOTION PASSED MOTION to adopt by resolution offered by Maloney second by Bonanno , that Teresa Egan be the Vice Chairperson of the Avon Planning Board beginning on January 1, 2022 and ending on the first meeting of the year 2023. THE VOTE: AYE : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO : 0 MOTION PASSED MOTION to adopt by resolution offered by Maloney second by Egan , that Sheila Sullivan be the Secretary for the Avon Planning Board beginning on January 1, 2022 and ending on the first meeting of the year 2023. THE VOTE: AYE : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO : 0 MOTION PASSED WHEREAS there exists the need for professional services of an attorney for the Planning Board of the Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea; NOW, THERFOR, BE IT RESOLVED by the Planning Board of Borough Avon-By-The-Sea that Mark G. Kitrick, Esq. be appointed Planning Board Attorney for the year 2022and shall continue to serve during the succeeding year until the 2023 Reorganization Meeting and be paid pursuant to a contract with Planning Board for services at a rate not to exceed $130.00 per hour. MOTION to adopt Resolution to appoint Mark G. Kitrick, Esq. as the Board attorney by Maloney second by Ernst . THE VOTE: AYE : : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO: 0 MOTION PASSED Whereas, there exists the need for professional services of an engineer for the Planning Board of Avon-By-The-Sea; NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Planning Board of the Borough of Avon-By- The-Sea that Raymond Savacool, P.E. be appointed Planning Board Engineer for the year 2022 and shall continue to serve the succeeding year until the 2023 Reorganization meeting and be paid pursuant to a contract with the Planning Board for services to be rendered at a rate not to exceed $135.00 per hour. AYE : : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO: 0 MOTION PASSED MEETING ADJOURNED OPEN REGULAR MEETING MOTION by Maloney second by Ryan to approve the December 9, 2021 Meeting Minutes as written THE VOTE : AYE: Bonanno, Maloney, Ryan, Talmage, Kenny, McLaughlin, McGovern, Mazouat NO : 0 ABSTAIN : Egan, Ernst, Davey MOTION PASSED MOTION by Ryan second by Bonanno to memorialize the Resolution denying the application by Chris Powers – 615 Fifth Avenue. THE VOTE : AYE : Bonnano, Ryan, Talmage, McGovern, Mazouat, McLaughlin ABSTAIN : 0 NO : 0 MOTION PASSED MOTION by Mahon, second by Kenny to memorialize the Resolution for Prati – 433 Sylvania Avenue. THE VOTE : AYE: Bonanno, Mahon, Ryan, Talmage, Kenny, McLaughlin, Mcgovern, Mazouat ABSTAIN : Maloney, Egan, Ernst, Davey MOTION PASSED NEXT on the agenda is a carried application for Francis & Jacqueline Mish – 335 Sylvania Avenue, from the November 18, 2021 meeting. Mr. Mish testifies about their previous application and hearing. Mr. Mish testifies to the revisions. Architect Tom Petersen testifies to the Plan revisions dated December 9, 2021. Board Engineer Ray Savacool addresses his review letter dated January 6, 2022. Comments from the public: Mr. Henry Burke of 710 Third Avenue states his approval of the Mish project. MOTION by Ernst second by Bonanno to approve the application with revisions. THE VOTE : AYE : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ernst, Talmage, Kenny, Mclaughlin, McGovern, ABSTAIN : Ryan, Davey, MOTION PASSED NEXT on the agenda is a new application for Fred & Laurie Schoenhut. The applicants are seeking Bulk variance relief and any and all other relief for property located at 310 Sylvania Avenue also known as Block 18 Lot 18. The applicant is proposing to demolish the single family residence and detached garage on a non-conforming lot and propose to build a new single family residence and detached garage. Mr. Mrs. Schoenhut are sworn in as is their Architect Mary Hearn, Attorney Michael Henderson, ESQ. and board Engineer Ray Savacool. Michael Henderson testifies to the application proposal. Mr. Schoenhut, the applicant testified in support of the application. Mr. Schoenhut states that the rear cottage will be rebuilt as a garage only. The home will be used for relatives and extended family when visiting Architect Mary Hearn is called to testify. Ms. Hearn refers to pictures of the existing property and dwellings ( shown on screen ). Ms. Hearn testifies to the pre-existing non conformities. Ms. Hearn describes the proposal as a clean slate to improve the property. No questions from the public or the board. Mr. Savacool ‘s revised review letter dated January 10, 2022 is discussed by Hearn and Savacool. Comments from the public? To board for comments and discussion. MOTION by Mahon second by Bonanno to approve the application. THE VOTE : AYE : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Davey, Kenny, McGovern ABSTAIN : McLaughlin noticed NO : Talmage MOTION IS PASSED NEXT on the agenda is a new application for Kenneth & Jennifer Sutphin, owners of 212 Lincoln Avenue, Block 33 Lot 15. The Sutphin’s propose to demolish the existing single family residence and construct a new 2 ½ story residence with front covered porch and rear uncovered exterior deck. The application references the need for a Building Height Variance where 35’ is required and 37’ is proposed. Existing garage and rear fence will remain. Attorney for the applicants Mark Aikens describes the proposal. Board attorney Mark Kitrick swears in Mr. & Mrs. Sutphin, architect Tom Petersen, engineer Brian Murphy and board engineer Ray Savacool. Mr. Petersen testifies to his architectural plans displayed on screen and describes the proposal. They are in a Flood Zone which triggers the 2’ Height variance. There will be no basement. Engineer/ Planner Brian Murphy testifies for the applicants. Mr. Murphy addresses parking, garage, setbacks, removal of some impervious to comply and overall coverage. Board Engineer Ray Savacool reads through his review letter dated August 13, 2022. Side platform discussed by board members and Petersen. Petersen states that the landing can be changed to 3’ from proposed 5’ if that is what the board desires. Board discussion ensues. MOTION by Maloney second by Mahon to approve the application with the change in landing Stipulated. THE VOTE : AYE : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Ernst, Talmage, Davey, Kenny NO : 0 MOTION PASSED MOTION by Maloney to adjourn second by Mahon. AYE : All Minutes approved at the March 10, 2022 Meeting. Motion by Maloney second by Bonanno to approve the January 25, Reorganization and Regular Meeting Minutes THE VOTE: AYE : Bonanno, Mahon, Maloney, Egan, Ryan, Kenny, McGovern NO : 0 MOTION PASSED Sheila Sullivan/ Planning Board Secretary

signals.evidence_excerpt: 1 Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea Regular Meeting – September 14, 2020 Pledge of Allegiance. Roll Call: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Mahon, Commissioner Magrini The Borough Clerk read the following statement: This is a regular meeting of the Board of Commissioners of the Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea and notice of time, date and place has been transmitted to the area newspapers, Avon Board of Education, Avon Public Library and the Avon Post Office in compliance with the rules and regulations of the Open Public Meetings Law. This meeting is conducted by means of communications equipment only, unless otherwise directed by state or local emergency management or health officials, consistent with Executive Order 103 (Murphy 392020). The public is directed to the Borough website at avonbytheseanj.com for information regarding phone access to the meeting. The public is asked to please mute their phones by either pressing the mute button on your phone or by pressing *6. The public will be allowed to unmute their phone either by disabling the mute on their phone or by pressing *6 for the public portion of the meeting. Work Shop Acting Clerk gave update on changes to the election. Mayor Bonanno gave update on the Food Drive taking place on Sept. 26. Commissioners discuss the closing of the beach for the weekend of Sept. 19th & 20th. Commissioners discuss moving the meetings to in-person & on the phone. Regular Meeting Motion by Commissioner Magrini, seconded by Commissioner Mahon that the minutes of the regular meeting of August 24, 2020 be approved as written. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Mayor Bonanno announced that this is the time advertised for second reading and hearing on Ordinance No. 17-2020 entitled: AN ORDINANCE OF THE BOROUGH OF AVON BY THE SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEWJERSEY, ADOPTING A REDEVELOPMENT PLAN FOR BLOCK 3, LOTS 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, AND 5.02; BLOCK 4.01, LOTS 1, 2, AND 3; BLOCK 4.02, LOTS 1, 2.01, AND LOTS 2.03 THROUGH 2.15, FIELD STREET AND SNYDER LANE WHEREAS, on January 14, 2019, the Mayor and Council of the Borough of Avon by the Sea (“Borough”) designated Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane as an “Area in Need of Rehabilitation” pursuant to the New Jersey Local Redevelopment and Housing Law, N.J.A.A. 40A:12A- 1, et seq (“LRHL”); and WHEREAS, the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law, N.J.S.A. 40A:12A-1 et seq. (“Redevelopment Law”), authorizes municipalities to determine whether certain parcels of land located therein constitute areas in need of redevelopment; and WHEREAS, pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40A-12A-4, the Borough, through the firm T&M Associates, prepared a redevelopment plan entitled “Northend Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane, ”substantially in the form attached hereto (“Redevelopment Plan”), which provides for the redevelopment of several areas of the Borough, specifically Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane; and 2 WHEREAS, prior to the final passage of this Ordinance, the Board of Commissioners referred the Redevelopment Plan to the Planning Board for its review at the August 13, 2020 meeting as to master plan consistency and for any other comments in accordance with N.J.S.A 40A:12A-7; and WHEREAS, the Redevelopment Plan has been available for public review in the office of the Planning Board and in the office of the Municipal Clerk; and WHEREAS, the Borough Council now wishes to approve and adopt the Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane, in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT ORDAINED by the Board of Commissioners of the Borough of Avon by the Sea, County of Monmouth, and State of New Jersey, that the Board of Commissioners hereby approves and adopts the Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, that the Redevelopment Plan shall supersede the applicable provisions of the Borough’s Zoning Ordinance. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, that the remainder of all other sections and subsections of the afore mentioned Ordinance not specifically amended by this Ordinance shall remain in full force and effect. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, all Ordinances or parts thereof inconsistent with the provisions of this Ordinance are hereby repealed as to such consistency. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, that this Ordinance shall take effect upon passage and publication in accordance with applicable law. EXHIBIT A Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane Mayor Bonanno asked if anyone from the public would like to be heard on the ordinance. Sharyn Mooney, 510 West End Ave., asked if the ordinance would address the brick building that used to have graffiti would be included. It does include that building however the owner will have to be the one to do something. There is a contract purchaser for Snyder’s property and hopefully once the rehabilitated it will encourage other property owners to do the same. Motion by Commissioner Magrini, seconded by Mayor Bonanno that the above Ordinance No. 17-2020 be approved on second reading and its passage published in a newspaper as required by law. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 88 - Review and Acceptance of 2019 Annual Audit: BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 88 ANNUAL AUDIT REPORT OF AUDIT FOR THE YEAR 2019 WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-4 requires the governing body of every local unit to have made an annual audit of its books, accounts and financial transactions, and WHEREAS, the Annual Report of Audit for the year 2019 as been filed by a Registered Municipal Accountant with the Municipal Clerk as per the requirements of N.J.S.A. 40A:5-6, and a copy has been received by each member of the governing body, and WHEREAS, the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey is authorized to prescribe reports pertaining to the local fiscal affairs, as per R.S. 52:27BB-34, and 3 WHEREAS, the Local Finance Board has promulgated a regulation requiring that the governing body of each municipality shall by resolution certify to the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey that all members of the governing body have reviewed, as a minimum, the sections of the annual audit entitled: GENERAL COMMENTS RECOMMENDATIONS WHEREAS, the members of the governing body have personally reviewed as a minimum the Annual Report of Audit, and specifically the sections of the Annual Audit entitled: GENERAL COMMENTS RECOMMENDATIONS as evidence by the group affidavit form of the governing body, and WHEREAS, such resolution of certification shall be adopted by the governing body no later than forty-five days after the receipt of the annual audit, as per the regulations of the Local Finance Board, and WHEREAS, all members of the governing body have received and have familiarized themselves with, at least, the minimum requirements of the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey, as stated aforesaid and have subscribed to the affidavit, as provided by the Local Finance Board, and WHEREAS, failure to comply with the promulgations of the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey may subject the members of the local governing body to the penalty provision of R.S. 52:27BB-52 - to wit: R.S. 52:27BB-52 - “A local officer or member of a local governing body who, after a date fixed for compliance, fails or refuses to obey an order of the director of Local Government Services, under the provisions of this Article, shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and, upon conviction, may be fined not more than on thousand dollars ($1,000.00) or imprisoned for not more than one year, or both, in addition shall forfeit his office.” NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the governing body of the Borough of Avon-By- The-Sea hereby states that it has complied with the promulgation of the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey dated July 30, 1968 and does hereby submit a certified copy of this resolution and the required affidavit to said Board to show evidence of said compliance. The Mayor and Commissioners commented that the audit was very good and there were no recommendations for the Borough to correct for next year. It will be posted on the website. Resolution No. 88 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Commissioner Magrini, review and acceptance of 2019 Annual Audit. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 89 - Authorizing the Mayor to sign the Monmouth County Open Space Program Grant application.: BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 89 AUTHORIZING THE MAYOR TO APPLY FOR THE MONMOUTH COUNTY OPEN SPACE GRANT WHEREAS, the Monmouth County Board of Chosen Freeholders has approved an Open Space Trust Fund and established a Municipal Open Space Program to provide Program Grant funds in connection with municipal acquisition of lands for County park, recreation, conservation and farmland preservation purposes, as well as for County recreation and conservation development and maintenance purposes; and WHEREAS, the Governing Body of Avon-by-the-Sea desires to obtain County Open Space Trust Funds in the amount of $15,000.00 to fund the Avon-by-the-Sea Recreational Facilities Improvements Project impacting Volunteer Park, located at South Station Ave., Tax Block 40.01, Lot 1 and the Avon Ballfield, located at 25 Main St., Tax Block 42, Lot 2; and WHEREAS, the total cost of the project including all matching funds is $30,000.00; and 4 WHEREAS, the Borough of Avon-by-the-Sea is the owner of and controls the project site. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY Avon-by-the-Sea Board of Commissioners THAT: 1. Edward Bonanno, Mayor or his/her successor is authorized to (a) make an application to the County of Monmouth for Open Space Trust Funds, (b) provide additional application information and furnish such documents as may be required for the Municipal Open Space Grants Program and (c) act as the municipal contact person and correspondent of the above named municipality; and 2. The Borough of Avon-by-the-Sea is committed to this project and will provide the balance of funding necessary to complete the project as described in the grant application in the form of non-county matching funds as required in the Policy and Procedures Manual for the Program; and 3. If the County of Monmouth determines that the application is complete and in conformance with the Monmouth County Municipal Open Space Program and the Policy and Procedures Manual for the Municipal Grants Program adopted thereto, the municipality is willing to use the approved Open Space Trust Funds in accordance with such policies and procedures, and applicable federal, state, and local government rules, regulations and statutes thereto; and 4. Edward Bonanno, Mayor or his/her successor is hereby authorized to sign and execute any required documents, agreements, and amendments thereto with the County of Monmouth for the approved Open Space Trust Funds; and 5. This resolution shall take effect immediately. Kerry McGuigan, Borough Administrator, presented the project scope summary as follows: The Borough is submitting an application to the 2020 Monmouth County Open Space Program. The grant is a 50% matching grant and can be used to expand and improve park and open space. This grant is to be used towards the Avon-by-the-Sea Recreational Facilities Improvements Project, impacting Volunteer Park, located at South Station Ave., Tax Block 40.01, Lot 1 and the Avon Ballfield, located at 25 Main Street, Tax Block 42, Lot 2. The project consists of the following: • Installing a new 12’ high fence along the Main Street side of the Avon Ballfield • Installing a new 4’ high fence along the east side of the playground area at Volunteer Park • Installing a new 6’ high fence along the west side of the playground area at Volunteer Park • Installing lawn sprinklers in the grass area surrounding the playground at Volunteer Park The cost for these improvements is $30,000 so the grant request is for $15,000 and the Borough will provide the matching funds. The Mayor and Commissioners discussed some areas this grant would help such as the Avon Volunteer Park and the ballfield. The grant is for $15,000 and the Borough will need to match that amount. Resolution No. 89 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Mayor Bonanno authorizing the Mayor to sign the Monmouth County Open Space Program Grant application. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 90 – Emergency Replacement of Motor in Well #2 BE IT RESOLVED, that the Board of Commissioners of the Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea hereby authorizes the emergency services of A.C. Schultes, Inc. for an emergency replacement of the motor in Well #2 at the water plant in an amount not to exceed $11,985.00 BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that a copy of this Resolution be forwarded to John Antonides, CFO and Thea Kratochvil, Borough Clerk. Resolution No. 90 offered by Mayor Bonanno, seconded by Commissioner Magrini to authorize the emergency replacement of the motor in Well #2 by A.C. Schultes. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon 5 Resolution No. 91 – Renewal of Liquor License for Norwood Inn, LLC BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 91 RENEWAL OF LIQUOR LICENSE NORWOOD INN, LLC WHEREAS, application has been made for a Seasonal Retail Consumption License No. 1305- 34-001-005 by Norwood Inn, LLC; and NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Board of Commissioners that Seasonal Retail Consumption License No. 1305-34-001-005 for the period from July 1, 2020 to June 30, 2021, dates inclusive, be renewed as an inactive license held in pocket for the fee of $1,500.00. BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Borough Clerk be and is authorized to sign and deliver license certificate to the said applicant. Resolution No. 92 – Renewal of Liquor License for the Avon Hotel Corp., T/A the Columns: BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 92 RENEWAL OF LIQUOR LICENSE THE AVON HOTEL CORP., T/A THE COLUMNS BE IT RESOLVED that the Board of Commissioners do hereby approve the renewal of the Seasonal Retail Consumption License No. 1305-34-004-003 for the Avon Hotel Corp., T/A the Columns at the fee of $1,500.00 for the period from July 1, 2020 to June 30, 2021, dates inclusive with the following conditions: 1. No amplifiers inside or outside that disturb neighbors. 2. No interior or exterior lighting be directed at neighbors. 3. No loud music. 4. No additional bars shall be constructed on premises. 5. The following dress code shall be enforced: No bathing suits after 7:00 p.m. 6. Any recommendations of the Fire Inspector and/or Hotel/Rooming House Inspector are complied with. 7. The Board of Commissioners have the right to restrict the operation of the porch. 8. Alcoholic beverages served with food service only on the porch. No new porch seating after 10:00 p.m. All porch service ceased by 11:00 p.m. 9. Raw bar/grille room will provide food & bar service as per submitted plans to the Borough. 10. Licensee must establish and maintain a smoking area outside their establishment on their own property. A map of said area must be submitted to the Borough office for approval. Resolution No. 93 – Renewal of Liquor License for Jai Mataji Avon, Inc. BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 93 RENEWAL OF LIQUOR LICENSE JAI MATAJI AVON, INC WHEREAS, application has been made for a Plenary Retail Distribution License No. 1305-44- 002-004 by Jai Mataji Avon Inc. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the Board of Commissioners do hereby approve the renewal of the Plenary Retail Distribution License No. 1305-44-002-004 for Jai Mataji Avon Inc. at the fee of $1,500.00 for the period from July 1, 2020 to June 30, 2021, dates inclusive. Resolution Numbers 91, 92, and 93 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Mayor Bonanno approving the renewal of liquor licenses for Norwood Inn, LLC, the Avon Hotel Corp., T/A the Columns, and Jai Mataji Avon, Inc. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 94 – Rescinding the opening of the beaches for September 19th and 20th as previously stated in Resolution No. 86. Resolution No. 94 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Commissioner Magrini to rescind opening the beaches for September 19th and 20th. 6 Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Motion by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Mayor Bonanno that the bills in the amount of $524,598.83 be approved for payment. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Department Head Reports Chief Haar – Since the August 10th meeting his department has had 971 calls, 178 stops, 36 details, and 52 borough ordinance reports. He would like to alert the public that there have been several bike thefts in town so residents should take care of storing their bicycles. Chief Haar thanked the Mayor, Commissioners and Public Works for all their hard work this summer with all its challenges. Scott Hauselt, DPW – The garbage tonnage is up and the recycling tonnage is down from last year. He wanted to remind residents that recycling must be loose and no plastic bags are allowed. Several residents are still putting plastic bags in their recycling and if it continues, he will send out letters reminding them that the borough can’t take it if it is in a plastic bag. Doug Carter, Recreation – gave update on Shore United Soccer; will re-visit having Leisures start in Jan.; Avon will be lead manager for basketball-program will be scaled down; still no groups allowed in gym; looking at events in pavilion and for Halloween. Sheila Watson, Librarian – starting Wed., 9/16 library will be open from 12N to 7 pm. Karl Klug, Water Supt. – will be flushing hydrants the end of Sept. or beginning of Oct. Ray Savacool, Engineer – gave update on 1st & 2nd Ave. project. Barry Cooke, Attorney – nothing to report. Mayor Bonanno asked if anyone from the public would like to be heard. No public comment. Motion by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Commissioner Magrini that being there is no further business, the meeting is adjourned. Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Meeting adjourned. Respectfully Submitted, Thea Kratochvil Municipal Clerk Avon-By-The-Sea CERTIFICATION I hereby certify the above to be a true and correct copy of Minutes from the meeting held by the Board of Commissioners of the Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea on September 14, 2020. _______________________ Thea Kratochvil Borough Clerk

signals.evidence_excerpt: 1 Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea Regular Meeting – September 14, 2020 Pledge of Allegiance. Roll Call: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Mahon, Commissioner Magrini The Borough Clerk read the following statement: This is a regular meeting of the Board of Commissioners of the Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea and notice of time, date and place has been transmitted to the area newspapers, Avon Board of Education, Avon Public Library and the Avon Post Office in compliance with the rules and regulations of the Open Public Meetings Law. This meeting is conducted by means of communications equipment only, unless otherwise directed by state or local emergency management or health officials, consistent with Executive Order 103 (Murphy 392020). The public is directed to the Borough website at avonbytheseanj.com for information regarding phone access to the meeting. The public is asked to please mute their phones by either pressing the mute button on your phone or by pressing *6. The public will be allowed to unmute their phone either by disabling the mute on their phone or by pressing *6 for the public portion of the meeting. Work Shop Acting Clerk gave update on changes to the election. Mayor Bonanno gave update on the Food Drive taking place on Sept. 26. Commissioners discuss the closing of the beach for the weekend of Sept. 19th & 20th. Commissioners discuss moving the meetings to in-person & on the phone. Regular Meeting Motion by Commissioner Magrini, seconded by Commissioner Mahon that the minutes of the regular meeting of August 24, 2020 be approved as written. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Mayor Bonanno announced that this is the time advertised for second reading and hearing on Ordinance No. 17-2020 entitled: AN ORDINANCE OF THE BOROUGH OF AVON BY THE SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEWJERSEY, ADOPTING A REDEVELOPMENT PLAN FOR BLOCK 3, LOTS 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, AND 5.02; BLOCK 4.01, LOTS 1, 2, AND 3; BLOCK 4.02, LOTS 1, 2.01, AND LOTS 2.03 THROUGH 2.15, FIELD STREET AND SNYDER LANE WHEREAS, on January 14, 2019, the Mayor and Council of the Borough of Avon by the Sea (“Borough”) designated Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane as an “Area in Need of Rehabilitation” pursuant to the New Jersey Local Redevelopment and Housing Law, N.J.A.A. 40A:12A- 1, et seq (“LRHL”); and WHEREAS, the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law, N.J.S.A. 40A:12A-1 et seq. (“Redevelopment Law”), authorizes municipalities to determine whether certain parcels of land located therein constitute areas in need of redevelopment; and WHEREAS, pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40A-12A-4, the Borough, through the firm T&M Associates, prepared a redevelopment plan entitled “Northend Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane, ”substantially in the form attached hereto (“Redevelopment Plan”), which provides for the redevelopment of several areas of the Borough, specifically Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane; and 2 WHEREAS, prior to the final passage of this Ordinance, the Board of Commissioners referred the Redevelopment Plan to the Planning Board for its review at the August 13, 2020 meeting as to master plan consistency and for any other comments in accordance with N.J.S.A 40A:12A-7; and WHEREAS, the Redevelopment Plan has been available for public review in the office of the Planning Board and in the office of the Municipal Clerk; and WHEREAS, the Borough Council now wishes to approve and adopt the Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane, in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT ORDAINED by the Board of Commissioners of the Borough of Avon by the Sea, County of Monmouth, and State of New Jersey, that the Board of Commissioners hereby approves and adopts the Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, that the Redevelopment Plan shall supersede the applicable provisions of the Borough’s Zoning Ordinance. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, that the remainder of all other sections and subsections of the afore mentioned Ordinance not specifically amended by this Ordinance shall remain in full force and effect. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, all Ordinances or parts thereof inconsistent with the provisions of this Ordinance are hereby repealed as to such consistency. BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED, that this Ordinance shall take effect upon passage and publication in accordance with applicable law. EXHIBIT A Redevelopment Plan for Block 3, Lots 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.01, and 5.02; Block 4.01, Lots 1, 2, and 3; Block 4.02, Lots 1, 2.01, and Lots 2.03 through 2.15, Field Street and Snyder Lane Mayor Bonanno asked if anyone from the public would like to be heard on the ordinance. Sharyn Mooney, 510 West End Ave., asked if the ordinance would address the brick building that used to have graffiti would be included. It does include that building however the owner will have to be the one to do something. There is a contract purchaser for Snyder’s property and hopefully once the rehabilitated it will encourage other property owners to do the same. Motion by Commissioner Magrini, seconded by Mayor Bonanno that the above Ordinance No. 17-2020 be approved on second reading and its passage published in a newspaper as required by law. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 88 - Review and Acceptance of 2019 Annual Audit: BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 88 ANNUAL AUDIT REPORT OF AUDIT FOR THE YEAR 2019 WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-4 requires the governing body of every local unit to have made an annual audit of its books, accounts and financial transactions, and WHEREAS, the Annual Report of Audit for the year 2019 as been filed by a Registered Municipal Accountant with the Municipal Clerk as per the requirements of N.J.S.A. 40A:5-6, and a copy has been received by each member of the governing body, and WHEREAS, the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey is authorized to prescribe reports pertaining to the local fiscal affairs, as per R.S. 52:27BB-34, and 3 WHEREAS, the Local Finance Board has promulgated a regulation requiring that the governing body of each municipality shall by resolution certify to the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey that all members of the governing body have reviewed, as a minimum, the sections of the annual audit entitled: GENERAL COMMENTS RECOMMENDATIONS WHEREAS, the members of the governing body have personally reviewed as a minimum the Annual Report of Audit, and specifically the sections of the Annual Audit entitled: GENERAL COMMENTS RECOMMENDATIONS as evidence by the group affidavit form of the governing body, and WHEREAS, such resolution of certification shall be adopted by the governing body no later than forty-five days after the receipt of the annual audit, as per the regulations of the Local Finance Board, and WHEREAS, all members of the governing body have received and have familiarized themselves with, at least, the minimum requirements of the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey, as stated aforesaid and have subscribed to the affidavit, as provided by the Local Finance Board, and WHEREAS, failure to comply with the promulgations of the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey may subject the members of the local governing body to the penalty provision of R.S. 52:27BB-52 - to wit: R.S. 52:27BB-52 - “A local officer or member of a local governing body who, after a date fixed for compliance, fails or refuses to obey an order of the director of Local Government Services, under the provisions of this Article, shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and, upon conviction, may be fined not more than on thousand dollars ($1,000.00) or imprisoned for not more than one year, or both, in addition shall forfeit his office.” NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the governing body of the Borough of Avon-By- The-Sea hereby states that it has complied with the promulgation of the Local Finance Board of the State of New Jersey dated July 30, 1968 and does hereby submit a certified copy of this resolution and the required affidavit to said Board to show evidence of said compliance. The Mayor and Commissioners commented that the audit was very good and there were no recommendations for the Borough to correct for next year. It will be posted on the website. Resolution No. 88 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Commissioner Magrini, review and acceptance of 2019 Annual Audit. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 89 - Authorizing the Mayor to sign the Monmouth County Open Space Program Grant application.: BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 89 AUTHORIZING THE MAYOR TO APPLY FOR THE MONMOUTH COUNTY OPEN SPACE GRANT WHEREAS, the Monmouth County Board of Chosen Freeholders has approved an Open Space Trust Fund and established a Municipal Open Space Program to provide Program Grant funds in connection with municipal acquisition of lands for County park, recreation, conservation and farmland preservation purposes, as well as for County recreation and conservation development and maintenance purposes; and WHEREAS, the Governing Body of Avon-by-the-Sea desires to obtain County Open Space Trust Funds in the amount of $15,000.00 to fund the Avon-by-the-Sea Recreational Facilities Improvements Project impacting Volunteer Park, located at South Station Ave., Tax Block 40.01, Lot 1 and the Avon Ballfield, located at 25 Main St., Tax Block 42, Lot 2; and WHEREAS, the total cost of the project including all matching funds is $30,000.00; and 4 WHEREAS, the Borough of Avon-by-the-Sea is the owner of and controls the project site. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY Avon-by-the-Sea Board of Commissioners THAT: 1. Edward Bonanno, Mayor or his/her successor is authorized to (a) make an application to the County of Monmouth for Open Space Trust Funds, (b) provide additional application information and furnish such documents as may be required for the Municipal Open Space Grants Program and (c) act as the municipal contact person and correspondent of the above named municipality; and 2. The Borough of Avon-by-the-Sea is committed to this project and will provide the balance of funding necessary to complete the project as described in the grant application in the form of non-county matching funds as required in the Policy and Procedures Manual for the Program; and 3. If the County of Monmouth determines that the application is complete and in conformance with the Monmouth County Municipal Open Space Program and the Policy and Procedures Manual for the Municipal Grants Program adopted thereto, the municipality is willing to use the approved Open Space Trust Funds in accordance with such policies and procedures, and applicable federal, state, and local government rules, regulations and statutes thereto; and 4. Edward Bonanno, Mayor or his/her successor is hereby authorized to sign and execute any required documents, agreements, and amendments thereto with the County of Monmouth for the approved Open Space Trust Funds; and 5. This resolution shall take effect immediately. Kerry McGuigan, Borough Administrator, presented the project scope summary as follows: The Borough is submitting an application to the 2020 Monmouth County Open Space Program. The grant is a 50% matching grant and can be used to expand and improve park and open space. This grant is to be used towards the Avon-by-the-Sea Recreational Facilities Improvements Project, impacting Volunteer Park, located at South Station Ave., Tax Block 40.01, Lot 1 and the Avon Ballfield, located at 25 Main Street, Tax Block 42, Lot 2. The project consists of the following: • Installing a new 12’ high fence along the Main Street side of the Avon Ballfield • Installing a new 4’ high fence along the east side of the playground area at Volunteer Park • Installing a new 6’ high fence along the west side of the playground area at Volunteer Park • Installing lawn sprinklers in the grass area surrounding the playground at Volunteer Park The cost for these improvements is $30,000 so the grant request is for $15,000 and the Borough will provide the matching funds. The Mayor and Commissioners discussed some areas this grant would help such as the Avon Volunteer Park and the ballfield. The grant is for $15,000 and the Borough will need to match that amount. Resolution No. 89 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Mayor Bonanno authorizing the Mayor to sign the Monmouth County Open Space Program Grant application. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 90 – Emergency Replacement of Motor in Well #2 BE IT RESOLVED, that the Board of Commissioners of the Borough of Avon-By-The-Sea hereby authorizes the emergency services of A.C. Schultes, Inc. for an emergency replacement of the motor in Well #2 at the water plant in an amount not to exceed $11,985.00 BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that a copy of this Resolution be forwarded to John Antonides, CFO and Thea Kratochvil, Borough Clerk. Resolution No. 90 offered by Mayor Bonanno, seconded by Commissioner Magrini to authorize the emergency replacement of the motor in Well #2 by A.C. Schultes. BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Borough Clerk be and is authorized to sign and deliver license certificate to the said applicant. Resolution No. 92 – Renewal of Liquor License for the Avon Hotel Corp., T/A the Columns: BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 92 RENEWAL OF LIQUOR LICENSE THE AVON HOTEL CORP., T/A THE COLUMNS BE IT RESOLVED that the Board of Commissioners do hereby approve the renewal of the Seasonal Retail Consumption License No. 1305-34-004-003 for the Avon Hotel Corp., T/A the Columns at the fee of $1,500.00 for the period from July 1, 2020 to June 30, 2021, dates inclusive with the following conditions: 1. No amplifiers inside or outside that disturb neighbors. 2. No interior or exterior lighting be directed at neighbors. 3. No loud music. 4. No additional bars shall be constructed on premises. 5. The following dress code shall be enforced: No bathing suits after 7:00 p.m. 6. Any recommendations of the Fire Inspector and/or Hotel/Rooming House Inspector are complied with. 7. The Board of Commissioners have the right to restrict the operation of the porch. 8. Alcoholic beverages served with food service only on the porch. Licensee must establish and maintain a smoking area outside their establishment on their own property. A map of said area must be submitted to the Borough office for approval. Resolution No. 93 – Renewal of Liquor License for Jai Mataji Avon, Inc. BOROUGH OF AVON-BY-THE-SEA, COUNTY OF MONMOUTH, STATE OF NEW JERSEY, RESOLUTION NO. 93 RENEWAL OF LIQUOR LICENSE JAI MATAJI AVON, INC WHEREAS, application has been made for a Plenary Retail Distribution License No. 1305-44- 002-004 by Jai Mataji Avon Inc. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the Board of Commissioners do hereby approve the renewal of the Plenary Retail Distribution License No. 1305-44-002-004 for Jai Mataji Avon Inc. at the fee of $1,500.00 for the period from July 1, 2020 to June 30, 2021, dates inclusive. Resolution Numbers 91, 92, and 93 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Mayor Bonanno approving the renewal of liquor licenses for Norwood Inn, LLC, the Avon Hotel Corp., T/A the Columns, and Jai Mataji Avon, Inc. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Resolution No. 94 – Rescinding the opening of the beaches for September 19th and 20th as previously stated in Resolution No. 86. Resolution No. 94 offered by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Commissioner Magrini to rescind opening the beaches for September 19th and 20th. 6 Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Motion by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Mayor Bonanno that the bills in the amount of $524,598.83 be approved for payment. Vote: Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Department Head Reports Chief Haar – Since the August 10th meeting his department has had 971 calls, 178 stops, 36 details, and 52 borough ordinance reports. He would like to alert the public that there have been several bike thefts in town so residents should take care of storing their bicycles. Chief Haar thanked the Mayor, Commissioners and Public Works for all their hard work this summer with all its challenges. Scott Hauselt, DPW – The garbage tonnage is up and the recycling tonnage is down from last year. He wanted to remind residents that recycling must be loose and no plastic bags are allowed. Several residents are still putting plastic bags in their recycling and if it continues, he will send out letters reminding them that the borough can’t take it if it is in a plastic bag. Doug Carter, Recreation – gave update on Shore United Soccer; will re-visit having Leisures start in Jan.; Avon will be lead manager for basketball-program will be scaled down; still no groups allowed in gym; looking at events in pavilion and for Halloween. Sheila Watson, Librarian – starting Wed., 9/16 library will be open from 12N to 7 pm. Karl Klug, Water Supt. – will be flushing hydrants the end of Sept. or beginning of Oct. Ray Savacool, Engineer – gave update on 1st & 2nd Ave. project. Barry Cooke, Attorney – nothing to report. Mayor Bonanno asked if anyone from the public would like to be heard. No public comment. Motion by Commissioner Mahon, seconded by Commissioner Magrini that being there is no further business, the meeting is adjourned. Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Commissioner Mahon Meeting adjourned.
